subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
1. The following is from the 2016 Republican Party Platform. 
“Because the Federal Reserve’s monetary policy decisions affect job creation, upward mobility for workers, 
and equitable prosperity, they should be transparent. Similarly, the Federal Reserve’s important role as a lender 
of last resort should also be carried out in a more transparent manner. The Republican Party will advance 
legislation that brings transparency and accountability to the Federal Reserve, the Federal Open Market 
Committee, and the Federal Reserve’s dealing with foreign banks.” 
https://www.gop.com/the-2016-republican-party-platform/ 
After reading the scenario, respond to A, B, and C below. 
A. Describe an action, other than advancing legislation, that members of Congress may take to bring 
transparency and accountability to the Federal Reserve. 
B. In the context of this section of the Republican Party platform, explain how the proposal to increase 
transparency and accountability described in part A would affect the Federal Reserve’s ability to 
make economic policy. 
C. Explain how ideological differences between the parties might affect Democratic Party support of the 
proposal.

of the Board of Governors of

information 
than stating that

a law to audit the

and accountability

Reserve’s ability to make

• The Federal Reserve would be

• The Federal Reserve would be

pressures when making

policy. 
Response should 
include more 
information than

Federal Reserve 
would be more 
transparent/

would lose its 
ability to make 
policies. 
Explain how ideological 
differences between 
the parties might affect 
Democratic Party 
support of the proposal.
• The Democratic Party believes 
in more regulation of the 
economy and thus would 
not support the proposal by 
Republicans, which might 
lead the Federal Reserve to be 
more attentive to Republican 
priorities which includes less 
regulation.
Response should 
include more 
information than 
stating that the 
Democratic Party 
believes in more 
regulation of the 
economy and thus 
would support the 
proposal.

Use the information graphic above to answer the following questions.
A. Describe a trend in presidential nominees to district courts with regard to gender.
B. Describe a similarity or difference between Republican and Democratic presidents’
nominees to federal district courts with regard to the political party of those they
nominate.
C. Explain how the similarity or difference affects the judicial decisions of the federal
district courts with regard to civil liberties.
D. Explain how the number and party affiliation of federal district court appointees
affects presidential influence and power.

Scoring Notes
Criteria 
Examples of types of responses 
that meet the criteria
Notes 
Describe a trend in 
presidential nominees 
to district courts with 
regard to gender. 
(1 point)
• The percentage of female 
appointees has generally 
increased over time. 
• Democrats tend to appoint a 
higher percentage of females 
than Republicans do. 
Responses that 
identify a data 
point but not a 
trend do not earn a 
point. 
Describe a similarity 
or difference between 
Republican and 
Democratic presidential 
nominees with regard 
to the political party of 
those they nominate. 
(1 point)
• One difference is that 
Democrats tend to nominate 
Democrats, and Republicans 
tend to nominate Republicans. 
• The parties are similar in that 
they nominate members of 
their own party. 
Response 
must compare 
Democrats and 
Republicans and 
cannot simply 
identify data.
Explain how the 
similarity or difference 
affects the judicial 
decisions of the federal 
district courts with 
regard to civil liberties. 
(1 point)
• Democratic appointees tend 
to support civil liberties in 
areas regarding privacy issues, 
suspect rights, and free speech 
cases. 
• Republicans appointees tend 
to support civil liberties on 
Second Amendment cases and 
religious freedom cases. 
Response must 
specify a result or 
an impact of the 
difference noted 
in the previous 
section of B. 
Explain how the 
number and party 
affiliation of judicial 
appointments affects 
presidential influence 
and power. (1 point) 
• Judicial appointees will likely 
support the same policies 
as the president. The more 
appointees that are appointed, 
the more likely it is that 
policies supported by the 
president will be upheld. 
• Presidents seek to appoint as 
many nominees as they can 
from their own party as a 
way to ensure a lasting legacy. 
Judicial appointees to the 
federal courts have lifetime 
tenure and have the ability 
to continue handing down 
decisions in line with the 
president’s party long after the 
president has served in office. 
Responses such as 
“Democratic will 
uphold democratic 
policies” is not 
sufficient because 
it doesn’t explain 
how the number 
of appointees 
affects presidential 
influence. 
Responses such 
as “The more 
appointments, the 
more power” do 
not adequately 
explain how the 
appointments 
increase power.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
3. In 2010 Arizona passed a law that sought to reduce the number of undocumented immigrants in the state. The 
law made it a crime to seek or obtain work in the state without proper documentation, and it also made hiring, 
sheltering, or transporting undocumented people illegal. It also gave local law enforcement the authority to 
require proof of residency in the course of a lawful arrest, and it gave them the authority to perform warrantless 
stops of people they suspected of being undocumented. 
The United States Department of Justice challenged the state law as an interference with the national 
government’s enumerated powers to regulate and enforce immigration law. In Arizona v. United States (2012), 
the Supreme Court agreed with the United States in a 6–3 decision stating most of the provisions of the law did 
conflict with federal authority. The Court said, “The Government of the United States has broad, undoubted 
power over the subject of immigration and the status of aliens. . . . This authority rests, in part, on the National 
Government’s constitutional power to establish an uniform Rule of Naturalization, and its inherent power as 
sovereign to control and conduct relations with foreign nations.” 
Based on the information above, respond to the following questions. 
A. Identify a common constitutional principle used to make a ruling in both McCulloch v. Maryland 
(1819) and Arizona v. United States (2012). 
B. Explain how the facts of McCulloch v. Maryland and the facts of Arizona v. United States led to a 
similar holding in both cases. 
C. Describe an action that Congress could take to respond to the Arizona v. United States decision if it 
disagreed with the decision.

Explain

Describe or explain an interaction 
between the holding in the non-
required Supreme Court case and 
a relevant political institution, 
behavior, or process. 
To earn this point,

demonstrate a grasp of 
the appropriate Enduring 
Understanding. 

Scoring Notes
Criteria 
Examples of types of responses 
that meet the criteria
Notes 
Identify a common 
constitutional principle 
used to make a ruling in 
both cases. (1 point)
• Supremacy clause 
Explain how the facts in 
McCulloch v. Maryland 
and Arizona v. United 
States led to a similar 
holding in both cases.
(2 points) 
• Describe the facts 
about McCulloch v. 
Maryland. (1 point) 
• Correctly explain 
how the facts of both 
cases led to the same 
holding. (1 point) 
• In McCulloch, the state of 
Maryland passed legislation to 
impose taxes on the nationally 
established bank. The Court 
ruled that federal government 
has the power to create a bank 
under its powers to collect 
taxes, borrow money, and 
regulate interstate commerce. 
The necessary and proper 
clause permits the federal 
government to pass any laws 
“necessary and proper” for its 
execution of these powers. It 
also found that “the power to 
tax, was the power to destroy,” 
and thus, Maryland’s tax 
violated the Supremacy Clause. 
• In Arizona, the state sought to 
pass a law about immigration 
enforcement that would 
undermine the federal 
government’s constitutional 
right to regulate and enforce 
immigration policy. The 
Court ruled in favor of the 
federal government’s power to 
interpret immigration policy 
under the Supremacy Clause. 
• In both cases, the court 
found in favor of the federal 
government, and the 
Supremacy Clause was used in 
the decision. 
Discussion must 
include the 
McCulloch case 
facts, and not just 
the holding, to earn 
the point. 
Discussion must 
also include how 
the supremacy 
clause or the 
necessary and 
proper clause 
was used in the 
ruling in favor 
of the national 
government. 
Describe an action that 
Congress could take to 
respond to the Arizona 
v. United States decision 
if it disagreed with the 
decision. (1 point) 
• Congress could pass a law 
granting the states more 
authority to determine how 
to regulate and enforce 
immigration laws.

classification.evidence: FRQ type and scoring guideline alignment from local 2018 full practice exam.

subject: AP United States Government and Politics
text:
4. 
Equality of opportunity is a central value in the American political system. Choose either Congress or the 
Supreme Court, and present an argument for why your choice is most responsible for advancing the principle 
of equality of opportunity in the United States. 
In your essay, you must: 
✓ Articulate a defensible claim or thesis that responds to the prompt and establishes a line of reasoning. 
✓ Support your claim with at least 
 
 
 
 
 TWO pieces of accurate and relevant information. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
• At least ONE piece of evidence must be from the list below. 
o Constitutional amendments 
o The Declaration of Independence
 
 
 
 
o Federalist 78 
• The second piece of evidence must be from a different source on the list above OR from your 
knowledge of Congress or the Supreme Court. 
✓ Use reasoning to explain why your evidence supports your claim/thesis. 
✓ Respond to an opposing or alternative perspective using refutation, concession, or rebuttal.

simply restating the 
prompt.
Evidence 
1 point
Provides 
one piece 
of evidence 
that is 
accurately 
linked to the 
topic of the 
question 
OR 2 points 
Uses one 
piece of 
specific and 
relevant 
evidence 
to support 
the claim or 
thesis
OR 3 points 
Uses two 
pieces of 
specific and 
relevant 
evidence 
to support 
the claim or 
thesis
To earn more than 
one point, the 
response must have a 
claim/thesis.
Reasoning 
1 point 
Explains how or why the evidence supports 
the claim or thesis 
The response must 
have a claim/thesis to 
earn a point. 
The response cannot 
simply restate the 
prompt to explain 
why the evidence 
supports the claim/
thesis. 
The response cannot 
earn a point without 
previously supporting 
the claim/thesis in B. 
To earn the point 
the response must 
address at least one 
piece of evidence. 
Responds to 
alternative 
perspectives 
1 point
Responds to an opposing or alternate 
perspective using refutation, concession, or 
rebuttal
The response must 
have a claim or thesis 
to earn a point. 
The response must 
identify an alternative 
perspective; 
demonstrate a correct 
understanding of 
the perspective; and 
refute, concede, or 
rebut the perspective. 

Scoring Notes
Criteria 
Examples of types of 
responses that would meet 
criteria 
Notes
A
Articulates a 
defensible claim 
or thesis that 
responds to the 
question and 
establishes line of 
reasoning 
(1 point) 
• “Congress/the Supreme 
Court is most responsible 
for advancing the 
principle of equality of 
opportunity because 
______.” 
The response cannot 
earn the point for thesis 
simply by picking 
Congress or the Supreme 
Court and repeating 
prompt that it “is most 
responsible for advancing 
the principle.”
B
Provides one (or 
more) piece(s) of 
evidence that is 
accurately linked 
to the topic of the 
question 
(1 point) 
• Correct statement/
evidence about Congress 
or the Supreme Court, but 
does not match model or 
thesis accurately.
The response cites 
a correct piece of 
information about the 
role of Congress or the 
Supreme Court but does 
not address the issue of 
equality of opportunity.
OR
B
Uses one piece of 
relevant evidence 
to support the 
claim or thesis 
(2 points) 
• Congress—Passing the 
14th amendment. 
• Federalist #78—Supreme 
Court and judicial review
• The Declaration of 
Independence—Congress 
is elected (government by 
consent).
• The Declaration of 
Independence—Supreme 
Court (court is independent 
of executive/a monarch)
The response cites 
a correct piece of 
information about how 
Congress or the Supreme 
Court advances equality 
of opportunity. 
OR
B
Uses two pieces of 
relevant evidence 
to support the 
claim or thesis 
(3 points) 
• “Congress has the power 
of the purse, so it can 
fund programs to advance 
equality.”
• “Members of Congress 
oftentimes interact directly 
with people and understand 
issues of inequality more 
than judges do.”
• “Members of the Supreme 
Court are not elected, and 
therefore, are impervious 
to public opinion when it 
opposes equality.”
Second piece of evidence 
may be from a student’s 
knowledge of Congress 
or the Supreme Court.

Criteria 
Examples of types of 
responses that would meet 
criteria 
Notes
C
Uses reasoning 
to explain why 
evidence supports 
claim/thesis
(1 point) 
• “Congress can pass 
amendments that 
foster equality, such as 
the 14th amendment, 
which guarantees 
each individual equal 
protection under the law 
and due process.” 
• “Federalist #78 argues in 
favor of judicial review, 
which the Supreme Court 
has used to evaluate 
the constitutionality 
of laws or actions that 
affect equality, such as 
in the Brown v. Board of 
Education decision, which 
struck down segregation 
in schools.” 
• “The Declaration of 
Independence declared 
that “all men are created 
equal” and that the powers 
of government are derived 
by consent. Congress 
is the only directly 
elected federal branch of 
government, making it the 
most likely to pass laws 
to uphold the principle 
of equality. Congress 
passed the Americans 
with Disabilities Act 
which allowed people with 
disabilities greater access 
to resources.”
The response cannot 
simply restate the prompt 
D
Responds to 
an opposing 
or alternative 
perspective 
using refutation, 
concession, or 
rebuttal (1 point) 
• The response correctly 
illustrates an opposing 
or alternative perspective 
(Congress/the Supreme 
Court) and responds to it. 
The response must 
be more than just 
acknowledging another 
view, such as “While 
some say the Congress/
Supreme Court best 
advances the principle of 
equality of opportunity, 
I say otherwise.”

Scoring Guidelines and Notes for Question 4
Course Framework Alignment
Disciplinary 
Practice(s)
Enduring Understanding/Learning Objective
Unit(s)
5.a
Articulate a 
defensible claim/
thesis.
5.b
Support the 
argument using 
relevant evidence.
5.c
Use reasoning 
to organize and 
analyze evidence, 
explaining its 
significance to 
justify the claim or 
thesis.
5.d
Use refutation, 
concession, 
or rebuttal in 
responding 
to opposing 
or alternate 
perspectives.
CON-3.A Describe the different structures, 
powers, and functions of each house of 
Congress.
CON-5.A Explain the principle of judicial 
review and how it checks the power of other 
institutions and state governments.
PRD-1.A Explain how constitutional 
provisions have supported and motivated social 
movements.
MPA-1.A Explain the relationship between core 
beliefs of U.S. citizens and attitudes about the 
role of government.
